rationale: Sahni (2016) is cited for a specific prior finding, not only in a background list: competitor spillovers can be positive at low ad intensity and weaken as intensity rises. The citing paper uses this idea to motivate its cross-brand advertising analysis and later returns to it when interpreting its own elasticity patterns versus share of voice and pairwise ad concentration. Because the cited study informs both framing and interpretation of core results, the citation is substantive.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Sahni (2016)'s spillover-by-intensity result and examines whether similar competitive spillover patterns appear in a broader multimedia, multibrand, and multichannel setting. It extends that line by estimating cross-brand elasticities across email, catalogs, and paid search and linking them to relative advertising concentration.
